name: Pecan Twists
method: Dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup warm water. Let rest until bubbles form on top. In a large bowl, combine flour and yeast, then add butter, sugar, milk, and eggs. Mix with a wooden spoon to form a rudimentary ball. Turn onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, or until dough forms a smooth ball. Drizzle olive oil on top and cover with a dish towel. Let rise in a warm place for 90 minutes. In the meantime, combine sugar, butter, cinnamon, vanilla, and chopped pecans in a small bowl to make a smooth paste. When dough is ready, punch down and turn onto a floured surface. With a rolling pin, roll out dough to 13 X 18 inches. Spread topping evenly, then fold along the longest side 1/2 way. Now fold the other side 1/2 way. Cut into 12 stripes. Cover and let rise for about an hour. In a small bowl, combine powdered sugar and milk. Twist each strip and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet 2 inches apart. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven and cool completely. Drizzle sugar mixture as pictured below. Makes 12 pecan twists.
